# php-via — Full LLM Reference

**Version**: latest (MIT) | **Requires**: PHP 8.4+, OpenSwoole extension
**Install**: `composer require mbolli/php-via`
**Homepage**: https://via.zweiundeins.gmbh/
**GitHub**: https://github.com/mbolli/php-via
**Packagist**: https://packagist.org/packages/mbolli/php-via
**Author**: Michael Bolli <michael@bolli.us>

---

## What is php-via?

php-via is a real-time reactive web framework for PHP. It runs as a persistent
OpenSwoole server that maintains long-lived SSE (Server-Sent Events) connections to
every browser tab. When state changes on the server, it pushes DOM patches to
connected clients via Datastar — a lightweight SSE library that uses the Declarative
Shadow DOM morphing algorithm. No JavaScript, no build step, multiplayer by default.

The name "via" means "the way". From a single PHP process you get full-duplex
real-time communication without WebSockets, polling, or any frontend framework.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
Browser ──── GET /page ────────────────► RequestHandler  (creates Context, renders HTML)
Browser ──── GET /_sse ────────────────► SseHandler      (SSE keep-alive, sends patches)
Browser ──── POST /_action/:id ────────► ActionHandler   (injects signals, calls handler)
                                              │
                                         Application
                                         ├── Router        (route matching, reflection DI)
                                         ├── Context       (per-tab: signals, actions, views)
                                         │   ├── PatchManager   (Channel → SSE queue)
                                         │   ├── SignalFactory
                                         │   ├── ComponentManager (sub-contexts)
                                         │   └── ContextLifecycle (timers, cleanup)
                                         └── Broker        (InMemory / Redis / NATS)
```

**Public API surface**: `Via`, `Context`, `Signal`, `Action`, `Scope`, `Config`

---

## Getting Started

### Requirements

- PHP 8.4 or higher
- OpenSwoole extension (`pecl install openswoole`)
- Composer
- Optional: `ext-brotli` for Brotli compression

### Installation

```bash
composer require mbolli/php-via
```

### Minimal counter app (complete working example)

```php
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

require __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php';

use Mbolli\PhpVia\Config;
use Mbolli\PhpVia\Context;
use Mbolli\PhpVia\Via;

$app = new Via(new Config());

$app->page('/', function (Context $c): void {
    $count = $c->signal(0, 'count');

    $c->action(function (Context $c) use ($count): void {
        $count->setValue($count->int() + 1);
        $c->sync();
    }, 'increment');

    $c->view(fn () => <<<HTML
        <main>
            <p data-text="\${$count->id()}">0</p>
            <button data-on-click="@post('/increment')">+1</button>
        </main>
    HTML);
});

$app->start(); // http://localhost:8080
```

---

## Core Concepts

### 1. Signals

Signals are reactive PHP values scoped to a connection, route, session, or the whole
server. Signal values are automatically serialised to the browser's Datastar store and
kept in sync.

```php
// Create a signal with initial value and optional name
$count  = $c->signal(0, 'count');        // integer
$name   = $c->signal('', 'name');        // string
$active = $c->signal(false, 'active');   // bool
$items  = $c->signal([], 'items');       // array → JSON

// Read values (typed)
$count->int();
$name->string();
$active->bool();
$items->array();
$count->getValue(); // mixed, raw

// Write a value
$count->setValue(42);
$count->setValue($count->int() + 1);

// Write and broadcast immediately (non-TAB scopes only)
$count->setValue(42, broadcast: true);  // default when scope != TAB

// Get the signal's Datastar store ID for use in Twig/HTML
$count->id(); // e.g. "count" or "ns.count" inside a component
```

Signal IDs are namespaced by the component hierarchy. Always use `$signal->id()` in
templates — never hard-code IDs.

### 2. Actions

Actions are server-side event handlers registered under a URL and invoked via Datastar
expressions in HTML (`@post(...)`, `@get(...)`, `data-on-click`, etc.).

```php
// Register with an explicit name → URL /action/increment
$inc = $c->action(function (Context $c) use ($count): void {
    $count->setValue($count->int() + 1);
    $c->sync(); // push updates to this tab only
}, 'increment');

// Get the URL for use in templates
$inc->url(); // "/action/increment?..."

// Signal injection: signal values from the browser are automatically injected
// as typed PHP arguments when parameter names match signal names
$greet = $c->action(function (Context $c, string $name): void {
    // $name is automatically populated from the "$name" Datastar signal
}, 'greet');

// Receive arbitrary input (non-signal)
$c->action(function (Context $c): void {
    $option = $c->input('option'); // reads from POST body or query string
}, 'vote');
```

### 3. Views

Views are closures (or template strings) that render HTML for a Context. They run on
initial page load and on every broadcast that affects this context.

```php
// Simple string view
$c->view('<p>Hello</p>');

// Twig template (string path, no closure overhead)
$c->view('pages/home.html.twig', ['name' => 'world']);

// Closure view — re-runs on every update
$c->view(function () use ($count, $twig): string {
    return $twig->render('counter.html.twig', ['count' => $count->int()]);
});

// Skip full-page re-render on SSE updates (when using components)
$c->view(function (bool $isUpdate) use ($c, $data): string {
    if ($isUpdate) {
        return ''; // components own their own patches
    }
    return $c->render('page.html.twig', $data);
});

// Only send a named Twig block on SSE updates (most efficient)
$c->view(fn () => $c->render('page.html.twig', $data), block: 'counter');

// Disable update caching for views that must always re-render fresh
$c->view('presence.html.twig', ['count' => count($app->getClients())], cacheUpdates: false);
```

### 4. Scopes

Scope controls which contexts share the same signals and receive broadcasts together.

| Scope | Who shares state |
|---|---|
| `Scope::TAB` (default) | Fully isolated per browser tab |
| `Scope::ROUTE` | All tabs on the same route (`/docs/signals`) |
| `Scope::SESSION` | All tabs in the same browser session |
| `Scope::GLOBAL` | Every connected client |
| `"room:lobby"` | Custom string — any group you define |
| `"room:*"` | Wildcard — matches all `room:…` scopes |

```php
// Set the scope for a context
$c->scope(Scope::GLOBAL);
$c->scope(Scope::ROUTE);
$c->scope(Scope::SESSION);
$c->scope('room:' . $roomId);

// Convenience helper for route-based scope
$c->scope(Scope::routeScope('/dashboard'));

// Add a second scope (context participates in multiple scopes)
$c->addScope(Scope::routeScope($c->getRoute()));

// Broadcast to a scope from anywhere
$app->broadcast(Scope::GLOBAL);
$app->broadcast('room:lobby');

// Broadcast from within a context (uses the context's own scope)
$c->broadcast();

// Sync only this tab (does not broadcast)
$c->sync();
```

### 5. Components

Components are isolated sub-contexts composed inside a parent context. Each component
has its own signals, actions, views, and a dedicated `<div id="c-{name}">` patch target.

```php
// Define a component closure
$counterComp = function (Context $c) use ($twig): void {
    $count = $c->signal(0, 'count');
    $inc = $c->action(fn (Context $c) => $count->setValue($count->int() + 1), 'inc');
    $c->view(fn () => $twig->render('components/counter.html.twig', [
        'count_id'  => $count->id(),
        'count_val' => $count->int(),
        'inc_url'   => $inc->url(),
    ]));
};

// Compose into a parent page
$app->page('/dashboard', function (Context $c) use ($counterComp, $twig): void {
    $counter = $c->component($counterComp, 'my-counter');
    $c->view(fn () => $twig->render('pages/dashboard.html.twig', [
        'counter' => $counter(),   // renders the component's initial HTML
    ]));
});
```

In the template, embed with:
```twig
<div id="c-my-counter">{{ counter | raw }}</div>
```

The `id="c-{name}"` div is the component's SSE update target. Via automatically
targets it when the component re-renders on broadcast.

---

## Broadcasting

```php
// Broadcast from a context (scope set via $c->scope(...))
$c->broadcast();

// Broadcast to a specific scope from anywhere
$app->broadcast(Scope::GLOBAL);
$app->broadcast(Scope::routeScope('/leaderboard'));

// Update one signal without broadcasting, then broadcast manually
$count->setValue(42, broadcast: false);
$c->broadcast();

// Broadcast with a debounce to collapse bursts
Timer::after(200, fn () => $app->broadcast(Scope::GLOBAL));
```

---

## Lifecycle

```php
// Server lifecycle
$app->onStart(function () use ($app): void {
    // runs once when the Swoole server boots
});

$app->onShutdown(function () use ($app): void {
    // graceful shutdown
});

// Per-client lifecycle
$app->onClientConnect(function (Context $c) use ($app): void {
    $app->broadcast(Scope::routeScope($c->getRoute()));
});

$app->onClientDisconnect(function (Context $c) use ($app): void {
    // cleanup or broadcast
});

// Process-wide recurring timer
$app->setInterval(function () use ($app): void {
    if (empty($app->getClients())) {
        return;
    }
    $app->broadcast(Scope::GLOBAL);
}, 1000); // every 1000 ms

// Context-level timer (tied to a single connection's lifecycle)
// Must be started inside the page handler or component
```

---

## Multi-node Scaling (Broker)

Out of the box php-via uses an in-memory broker (single process). For multi-node
deployments, swap in Redis or NATS:

```php
use Mbolli\PhpVia\Broker\RedisBroker;
use Mbolli\PhpVia\Broker\NatsBroker;

// Redis
$config->withBroker(new RedisBroker('redis://127.0.0.1:6379'));

// NATS (JetStream)
$config->withBroker(new NatsBroker('nats://127.0.0.1:4222'));
```

All `broadcast()` calls are transparently forwarded through the broker. Signals and
views stay per-node; only broadcast messages cross node boundaries.

---

## Config Reference

```php
$config = (new Config())
    ->withHost('0.0.0.0')
    ->withPort(8080)
    ->withTemplateDir(__DIR__ . '/templates')
    ->withStaticDir(__DIR__ . '/public')     // serves static files
    ->withDevMode(true)                       // verbose logging, no cache headers
    ->withBrotli()                            // requires ext-brotli
    ->withH2c()                               // HTTP/2 cleartext (behind TLS proxy)
    ->withCertificate($crt, $key)             // enable HTTPS directly
    ->withLogLevel('debug')                   // debug | info | warning | error
    ->withBroker(new RedisBroker(...))        // multi-node broker
    ->withBasePath('/app/')                   // set at startup when mounted at a sub-path;
                                              // throws InvalidArgumentException for invalid paths
                                              // (absolute URLs, protocol-relative, etc.)
                                              // exposes {{ basePath }} to all Twig templates
;
```

---

## Middleware

php-via supports PSR-15 middleware for authentication, rate-limiting, CORS, etc.

```php
// Global middleware (runs on every request)
$app->middleware(new CorsMiddleware([...]));

// Per-route middleware (chained on RouteDefinition)
$app->page('/admin', $handler)->middleware(new AuthMiddleware());

// Per-group middleware
$app->group('/admin', function (Via $app) use ($handler): void {
    $app->page('/dashboard', $handler);
    $app->page('/users',     $handler);
})->middleware(new AuthMiddleware());
```

---

## Twig Integration

`Via::getTwig()` returns the Twig environment for registering custom extensions and
runtime loaders. Variables automatically injected into every template via `Context::render()`:

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `contextId` | The connection's unique ID (used in `data-signals`) |
| `currentRoute` | The matched route pattern, e.g. `/docs/signals` |
| `basePath` | Base URL path, always ends with `/` (default: `/`) |
| `siteUrl` | Your configured site URL (set via `$twig->addGlobal(...)`) |
| `assetVersion` | Cache-busting version token (set via `addGlobal`) |

Key `data-*` attributes for Datastar in templates:
```html
<!-- Display a signal value -->
<span data-text="$count">0</span>

<!-- Two-way bind an input to a signal -->
<input data-bind="name" type="text">

<!-- Trigger an action on click -->
<button data-on-click="@post('{{ inc_url }}')">+1</button>

<!-- Show/hide based on signal -->
<div data-show="$loggedIn">Protected content</div>

<!-- Class toggle -->
<div data-class-active="$isActive">...</div>
```

---

## Comparisons

| Feature | php-via | Livewire | htmx + Alpine | Inertia.js | Phoenix LiveView |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Language | PHP | PHP (Laravel) | PHP (any) | PHP + JS | Elixir |
| Real-time push | ✅ SSE | ⚠️ needs Reverb | ❌ poll only | ❌ | ✅ WebSocket |
| Multiplayer scopes | ✅ built-in | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ PubSub |
| Zero JavaScript | ✅ | ❌ Alpine | ❌ Alpine/JS | ❌ React/Vue | ✅ |
| No build step | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Framework required | ❌ | Laravel | ❌ | Laravel/Symfony | Phoenix |
| Multi-server scaling | ✅ Redis/NATS | ⚠️ | N/A | N/A | ✅ |
| Persistent connection | ✅ SSE | ❌ HTTP | ❌ HTTP | ❌ HTTP | ✅ WS |

**Choose php-via when**: you want real-time multiplayer UIs in plain PHP with no
JavaScript or build tooling, or you need scoped broadcasts (route/session/global) without
an external pub/sub system.

**Choose Livewire when**: you're already in the Laravel ecosystem and don't need
server-pushed real-time without Reverb.

**Choose htmx when**: you're progressively enhancing an existing app and don't need
persistent connections or server-initiated updates.

**Choose Phoenix LiveView when**: you work in Elixir and want the mature, battle-tested
original inspiration for php-via.

---

## Common Pitfalls (Quick Reference)

1. **Broadcast sends stale data**: read from `$app->globalState()` inside view closures
   instead of per-client signal values.
2. **Dual state (globalState + Signal)**: scoped signals are already shared — drop
   `globalState` and just use `$c->scope(Scope::ROUTE); $s = $c->signal(...)`.
3. **Wrong signal ID in HTML**: always use `$signal->id()` in PHP and pass to template;
   never hard-code signal IDs.
4. **Full-page patch overwrites component updates**: return `''` from page view when
   `$isUpdate === true`; or use `block:` parameter on `$c->view()`.
5. **Variable null in view closure**: add it to both outer and inner `use` lists.
6. **Stale cached view after broadcast**: pass `cacheUpdates: false` to `$c->view()`.
7. **PatchElementsNoTargetsFound**: the root element in your rendered block must have
   a unique `id` attribute.
8. **Partial update replaces whole page**: your block root element's `id` collides with
   the shell's `<main id="content">`; give it a unique ID.
9. **Timer fires with no clients**: guard `$app->broadcast()` with
   `if (empty($app->getClients())) return;`.
10. **Shell templates ignore Twig**: shell HTML files use `str_replace` placeholders,
    not Twig; put layout logic in proper `{% extends %}` / `{% block %}` templates.
11. **Action returns HTTP 400**: stale `via_ctx` after server restart; refresh the page,
    or include `via_ctx` explicitly in custom `@post()` payloads.

---

- Packagist: https://packagist.org/packages/mbolli/php-via
- Changelog: https://github.com/mbolli/php-via/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md
- Datastar (SSE/DOM library): https://data-star.dev/
- OpenSwoole: https://openswoole.com/
